data.title: German Industrial Output Growth Beats Estimates
data.description:
Germany’s industrial production jumped 3.0% month-over-month in March 2025, rebounding from a 1.3% decline in February and surpassing market expectations of a 0.8% increase.

It marked the second rise this year and the strongest monthly gain since October 2021.

The recovery was driven by higher output in the automotive sector (8.1%), pharmaceuticals (19.6%), machinery and equipment (4.4%), and construction (2.1%).

In contrast, energy production fell by 1.8%.

Meanwhile, output in energy-intensive industries rose by 1.5%.

The less volatile three-month average showed industrial production grew by 1.4% in Q1 compared to the previous quarter.

On an annual basis, output declined by 0.2%, a significant improvement from a 4.0% drop in February.

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Dabur India's Price Target to INR590 From INR660, Keeps at Buy
German Exports at 11-Month High
data.description:
Exports from Germany increased by 1.1% month-over-month to an eleven-month high of EUR 133.2 billion in March 2025, following a 1.8% gain in the previous month.

Shipments increased to EU (3.1%), particularly the Euro area (3.8%) and non-Euro area (1.6%).

Meanwhile, shipments declined to third countries (-1.1%), mainly due to lower sales to the UK (-2.8%).

However, shipments to the US increased by 2.4% ahead of the US tariff enactment, and to China by 10.2%.

From January to March, the country’s exports rose by 0.9% to EUR 349.3 billion compared to the same period a year earlier.

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Ecovacs Robotics' Price Target to 48 Yuan From 46 Yuan, Keeps at Hold
Kinetik Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Kinetik Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 5 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 12 cents. The mean expectation of nine analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 32 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 13 cents to 46 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 29.8% to $443.26 million from a year ago; analysts expected $438.77 million.

  • Kinetik Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 5 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $6.13 million.

  • Kinetik Holdings Inc shares had fallen by 22.3% this quarter and lost 28.9% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 25.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, seven analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the oil & gas transportation services peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Kinetik Holdings Inc is $57.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 06:15 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: German Industrial Production Climbed in March Ahead of Tariffs
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Industrial production in Germany jumped in March, helped by U.S. firms stockpiling imported goods as they prepared for the Trump administration's tariffs.

Output grew 3.0% on month in March, German statistics agency Destatis said Thursday, swinging from a 1.3% fall in February. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a smaller 0.8% uptick.

Production was 1.4% higher in the first quarter of 2025 than in the last three months of 2024, the largest quarterly increase since the start of 2022, Destatis said, before energy prices skyrocketed after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

The boost in production came ahead of tariffs announced in April by President Trump. The U.S. imposed 10% import duties on most goods imports, alongside 25% tariffs on autos, steel and aluminum. Exports of goods to the U.S. from Germany were up 2.4% in March compared with February, separate Destatis data said Thursday.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: EDP Renovaveis 1Q Rev EUR763M
EDP Renovaveis 1Q EBITDA EUR476M
UK House Price Growth Surpasses Expectations
data.description:
The Halifax House Price Index rose 3.2% year-on-year in April 2025, following a revised 2.9% increase in March, and exceeding forecasts of a 2.6% rise.

This marks the fastest annual growth rate so far this year.

On a monthly basis, house prices grew by 0.3%, recovering from a 0.5% decline in March.

However, on a quarterly basis, the index dipped slightly by 0.1%, reflecting continued market stability.

The average property price rose to £297,781 from £296,899 in March. “Our latest data shows UK house prices increased by just under £900 in April,” said Amanda Bryden, Head of Mortgages at Halifax. “This continues a trend of remarkable price stability over the past six months.” She noted that mortgage affordability has improved as borrowing rates fall below 4%, while wage growth remains strong. “Despite a subdued economic climate and geopolitical risks, the housing market is demonstrating resilience, and we anticipate modest price growth to persist throughout the year.”

data.title: Sasco Board Approves Restructuring of Two Subsidiaries
data.description:
Saudi Automotive Services' (SASE:4050), d/b/a Sasco, board on Wednesday approved restructuring two subsidiaries, according to a Thursday release.

The decision includes transferring the affiliation of Saudi Automobile and Touring Club and Al Nakhla Al Oula Contracting Co. to Ostool Al Naqil Co. and Sasco Petroleum Services Stations Co., respectively.

The Saudi Arabian car services and fuel station operator said the process will enhance the performance of the company as well as its subsidiaries.

data.title: Nordex Lands 49-MW Turbine Order for German Wind Farm
data.description:
Nordex secured an order for its wind turbines totaling 49 megawatts from Getec green energy, according to a Thursday release.

The German wind company's order covers seven N163/6.X turbines, which will be installed at the Zerbst wind farm in Saxony-Anhalt, Germany, starting in the summer of 2026. The deal also includes a 20-year premium service agreement.

data.title: Sorrell's ad group S4 Capital posts 11% fall in first-quarter revenue
data.description: Martin Sorrell's ad group S4 Capital reported an 11.4% drop in first-quarter revenue on Thursday as its clients in the technology sector prioritised spending on artificial intelligence over marketing.

data.title: UK house prices rose unexpectedly in April - Halifax
data.description:
British house prices rose unexpectedly in April, figures from mortgage lender Halifax showed on Thursday.

Halifax said house prices rose by 0.3% in April after a 0.5% fall in March. A Reuters poll of economists had pointed to a fall of 0.1% for April.

House prices were 3.2% higher on the year - higher than the 2.6% rise forecast in the poll.
